home.subtitle: Klistra in en tweet-länk. Välj format. Ladda ner på några sekunder. Inget att installera.

items.q: Hur laddar jag ner en Twitter-video?
items.a: Öppna tweeten, tryck på Dela och sedan Kopiera länk. Klistra in den här och tryck på Ladda ner. Du får en MP4 — samma kvalitet som Twitter levererade. Det är hela flödet.

items.q: Hur laddar jag ner en Twitter-video på iPhone?
items.a: Kopiera tweet-länken i Safari eller X-appen. Klistra in den här. Tryck på Ladda ner. Håll fingret på filen och välj "Spara i Files", eller använd delningsmenyn för att skicka den till Photos.

items.q: Hur laddar jag ner en Twitter-video på Android?
items.a: Kopiera länken från X-appen. Klistra in den här. Tryck på Ladda ner. De flesta webbläsare lägger MP4:n i /Download — öppna den från Files eller galleriet.

items.q: Hur sparar jag en Twitter-video till kamerarullen?
items.a: På iOS: ladda ner MP4:n, öppna den, tryck på dela, välj Spara video. På Android: filer hamnar i /Download som standard — håll fingret på och flytta till galleriet om det behövs.

items.q: Är den här Twitter-nedladdaren säker?
items.a: Ja. Vi frågar inte efter din inloggning. Vi sparar inte det du laddar ner. Vi spårar inte vem som laddade ner vad. Det är en länkparser plus en hämtare — inget mer.

items.q: Kan jag få videon i HD?
items.a: Om den som postade gjorde det i HD får du HD — upp till 1080p, ibland högre. Ingen omkodning, ingen försämring.

items.q: Vart tar de nedladdade filerna vägen?
items.a: Dit din webbläsare brukar spara saker. iPhone Safari → Files → Nedladdningar. Chrome på Android → /Download. Dator → din mapp Nedladdningar.

items.q: Måste jag betala eller registrera mig?
items.a: Nej. Inget konto, ingen e-post, inget kort. Obegränsade nedladdningar. Annonserna håller det gratis.

mp3.title: Twitter till MP3 — Konvertera Twitter-video till MP3 online
mp3.description: Gratis Twitter till MP3-konverterare. Plocka ut ljudet från vilken tweet som helst med video och spara som MP3. Funkar på iPhone, Android och dator.
mp3.subtitle: Dra ut ljudet från vilken Twitter-video som helst. Spara som MP3 — rent, taggbart, redo för din podd-app eller spellista.
mp3.introHeading: När du bara vill ha ljudet
mp3.introBody: Ibland spelar bilden ingen roll — du vill ha talet, musiken, ögonblicket. Klistra in en tweet, få en MP3. Den spelas där en telefon eller laptop spelar ljud.
mp3.introBody2: Vi extraherar ljudspåret direkt. Ingen omkodning i högre bitrate än källan — det du får motsvarar det Twitter levererade.

items.q: Hur konverterar jag en Twitter-video till MP3?
items.a: Kopiera tweet-länken. Klistra in den här. Tryck på Konvertera till MP3. Du får en ljudfil — vanligtvis 128 till 320 kbps, beroende på vad originalvideon innehöll.

items.q: Matchar MP3-kvaliteten originalet?
items.a: Den matchar det ljud Twitter levererade. Vi extraherar — vi uppskalar inte. Var källan 192 kbps är det det du får.

items.q: Kan jag konvertera en tweet utan video?
items.a: Twitter lagrar inte rena ljudfiler. Så om tweeten bara har text eller bilder finns det inget ljud att plocka ut. Tweeten behöver en video för att det ska fungera.

items.q: Fungerar det här på iPhone?
items.a: Ja. Klistra in länken, tryck på Konvertera, spara sedan MP3:n i Files eller dela den till en musikapp som VLC eller Documents.

image.title: Twitter Image Downloader — Ladda ner Twitter-foto i HD
image.description: Gratis Twitter image downloader. Spara vilket foto som helst från X i full originalupplösning. Ingen vattenstämpel. Upp till fyra bilder per tweet.
image.subtitle: Snappa upp vilket foto som helst från en tweet — full originalupplösning, ingen vattenstämpel. Upp till fyra bilder per tweet, paketerade som en zip.
image.introHeading: Full storlek, precis som det laddades upp
image.introBody: Twitters app och webbplats renderar om bilder för att passa din skärm — de är mindre än det som faktiskt postades. Genom oss får du originalfilen. Samma dimensioner, samma komprimering, samma EXIF som den som laddade upp skickade.
image.introBody2: Vi vattenstämplar inte, nedskalar inte, tar inte bort metadata. Filen du sparar är den fil Twitter tog emot.

items.q: Hur sparar jag en Twitter-bild i full storlek?
items.a: Klistra in tweet-länken ovan. Vi hämtar varje foto i sin originalupplösning — exakt det som postades, ingen komprimering tillagd av oss.

items.q: Kan jag ladda ner alla fyra bilderna samtidigt?
items.a: Ja. Om tweeten har flera foton får du dem i en zip. Eller välj en i taget från förhandsvisningen.

items.q: Kommer filen att ha en vattenstämpel?
items.a: Nej. Inget stämplat på bilden. Det du sparar är råfilen.

items.q: Fungerar det för privata eller raderade tweets?
items.a: Bara publika, aktiva tweets. Är kontot låst eller tweeten raderad finns det inget att hämta.

about.title: Om oss — TwitDownloader
about.description: Om TwitDownloader: ett gratis verktyg utan registrering för att spara videor, GIF-filer, bilder och ljud från Twitter (X).
about.lead: Vi byggde det här för att varje existerande Twitter-nedladdare kändes som den var gjord 2010. Långsam, full av annonser, packad med nedladdningsknappar som faktiskt inte laddar ner.

sections.heading: Vad vi gör
sections.body: Klistra in en tweet-länk. Få tillbaka filen — MP4, MP3, foto eller GIF. Det är allt. Inget konto, ingen app, inget kort.

sections.heading: Vad vi inte gör
sections.body: Vi lagrar inte innehållet. Vi sparar inga loggar över vad du laddar ner. Vi säljer inte dina data. Vi ber dig inte registrera dig för att "låsa upp HD".

sections.heading: Hur det förblir gratis
sections.body: Annonser. Några diskreta annonsplatser på sidan. Det betalar servrarna. Det är hela affärsmodellen.

contact.title: Kontakt — TwitDownloader
contact.description: Kom i kontakt med TwitDownloader-teamet. Buggrapporter, funktionsförslag och upphovsrättsfrågor.
contact.lead: Om något är trasigt, eller om du sett en tweet vi inte borde spara, eller om du bara vill säga hej — skriv till oss.
contact.copyrightBody: Om en nedladdning bryter mot dina rättigheter, mejla oss med tweet-URL:en och bevis på ägarskap. Vi tar bort den cachade filen inom 24 timmar — oftast snabbare.

terms.title: Användarvillkor — TwitDownloader
terms.description: Användarvillkor för TwitDownloader. Läs dessa villkor innan du använder tjänsten.
terms.lead: Genom att använda TwitDownloader godkänner du följande. Vanlig svenska nedan.

sections.heading: 1. Vad vi erbjuder
sections.body: Ett verktyg för att hämta offentligt postat media från Twitter (X) för personligt bruk. Vi lagrar inte media själva — vi hämtar det Twitter levererar, på begäran.

sections.heading: 2. Ditt ansvar
sections.body: Du ansvarar för vad du gör med nedladdade filer. Respektera upphovsrätt. Återpublicera inte utan tillstånd. Sprid inte privat innehåll.

sections.heading: 3. Inte ansluten till X
sections.body: TwitDownloader är inte ansluten till, godkänd av eller sponsrad av X Corp. eller Twitter, Inc. Namnen "Twitter" och "X" är varumärken som tillhör sina respektive ägare.

sections.heading: 4. Ingen garanti
sections.body: Tjänsten tillhandahålls i befintligt skick. Vi försöker hålla den uppe, men vi lovar inte drifttid eller korrekthet. Förlita dig inte på den för något verksamhetskritiskt.

sections.heading: 5. Ändringar
sections.body: Vi kan uppdatera dessa villkor. Fortsatt användning efter en ändring innebär att du accepterar den. Väsentliga uppdateringar noteras högst upp.

privacy.title: Integritetspolicy — TwitDownloader
privacy.description: TwitDownloaders integritetspolicy. Vi ber inte om personuppgifter. Vi lagrar inte dina nedladdningar. Här är vad vi samlar in.
privacy.lead: Kortversionen: vi frågar inte efter ditt namn, din e-post eller din inloggning. Vi lagrar inte de videor du laddar ner. Längre version nedan.

sections.heading: Vad vi samlar in
sections.body: Vanliga serverloggar (IP-adress, webbläsartyp, referrer) för säkerhet och förebyggande av missbruk. De roteras regelbundet. Vi loggar inte vilka tweets du laddar ner eller försöker ladda ner.

sections.heading: Cookies
sections.body: Vi använder cookies för: språkinställning, temainställning och annonspersonalisering (via Google AdSense). Du kan stänga av cookies i din webbläsare utan att sidan slutar fungera.

sections.heading: Google AdSense
sections.body: Vi använder Google AdSense för att visa annonser. Google kan använda cookies för att personalisera annonser baserat på dina besök på vår sida och andra sidor. Avregistrera på https://www.google.com/settings/ads.

sections.heading: Analys
sections.body: Vi använder integritetsvänlig analys för att räkna sidvisningar. Ingen enskild användare identifieras. Inga data säljs.

sections.heading: Dina rättigheter
sections.body: Är du i EU, Storbritannien eller Kalifornien har du rätt att få tillgång till, rätta eller radera personuppgifter vi har om dig. Eftersom vi inte ber om personuppgifter finns det oftast inget att komma åt — men har du frågor, mejla oss.

sections.heading: Barn
sections.body: Vi samlar inte medvetet in data från någon under 13 år. Tror du att ett barn har gett oss data, kontakta oss så raderar vi den.

sections.heading: Ändringar
sections.body: Uppdaterar vi denna policy ändras datumet nedan. Väsentliga ändringar noteras.

gif.title: Twitter GIF Downloader — Spara GIF från Twitter som MP4
gif.description: Gratis Twitter GIF downloader. Spara vilken GIF som helst från X. Twitter levererar GIF-filer som tyst MP4 — det är det du får, i mindre och mjukare filer.
gif.subtitle: Spara vilken GIF som helst från Twitter. Obs: Twitter har lagrat GIF-filer som tyst MP4 sedan 2014 — det är det du kommer att få. Liten fil, mjuk uppspelning.
gif.introBody: När någon trycker på "Lägg till GIF" på Twitter konverterar plattformen animationen till en tyst MP4 i bakgrunden. Så när vi sparar en Twitter-GIF får du en MP4 — samma animation, mycket mindre fil, spelas överallt där video spelas.
gif.introBody2: Behöver du specifikt en .gif-fil kan du konvertera MP4:n med vilken gratis konverterare som helst. Men MP4 är oftast det bättre valet — mindre, mjukare, lättare att dela.

items.q: Varför får jag en MP4 istället för en riktig .gif-fil?
items.a: Twitter konverterar varje GIF till MP4 på serversidan. Det finns ingen original-.gif på deras servrar — bara MP4:n. Vi ger dig det som finns.

items.q: Hur laddar jag ner en GIF från Twitter?
items.a: Kopiera tweet-länken, klistra in den ovan, tryck på Hämta GIF. Du får en MP4. Den loopar precis som original-GIF:en gjorde.

items.q: Kan jag konvertera MP4:n tillbaka till en riktig .gif?
items.a: Ja — flera gratis verktyg online gör det. Men ärligt talat: MP4 är mindre, mjukare och fungerar där en GIF skulle. De flesta bryr sig inte.

items.q: Fungerar det på telefon?
items.a: Ja, iOS och Android. Klistra in länken, tryck på Hämta GIF, spara MP4:n i Files eller ditt galleri.

iphone.title: How to Download Twitter Video on iPhone — Step by Step
iphone.description: Step-by-step guide to download Twitter (X) video on iPhone. Save to Camera Roll, Files, or share to other apps. Works on iOS 15 and up. No app needed.
iphone.h1: How to download Twitter video on iPhone
iphone.subtitle: Save any tweet's video straight to your iPhone — no app, no shortcut, no jailbreak. Three taps and it's in your Files or Photos.
iphone.intro: Twitter doesn't let you save videos directly from its app on iPhone. The native Share menu has no Save Video option for someone else's tweet. The workaround is to copy the link, paste it into a downloader, and save the MP4 to Files or Photos. Below: the exact steps, plus how to bypass common iOS quirks.

sections.heading: Step 1 — Copy the tweet link in the X app or Safari
sections.body: Open the tweet you want to save. Tap the Share icon (paper plane in the X app, arrow box in Safari). Choose Copy link. The URL is now on your clipboard — it should look like https://x.com/username/status/....

sections.heading: Step 2 — Paste the link on TwitDownloader
sections.body: Open this site in Safari (or Chrome). Tap the input box on the homepage. Hold and pick Paste. Hit Download. Within a couple of seconds you'll see preview thumbnails and quality options.

sections.heading: Step 3 — Save the MP4 to Files or Photos
sections.body: Tap the HD button. Safari downloads the MP4. Tap the small download icon (next to the address bar) → tap the file → Share icon → Save Video to put it in Photos, or Save to Files for the Files app. Either way it's now offline.

sections.heading: Common iPhone gotchas
sections.body: If Save Video is missing from the share sheet, you opened the file in the wrong app — open the download from Files first. If Safari blocks the download, check Settings → Safari → Downloads and make sure storage is set to On My iPhone or iCloud Drive. If the file plays inline instead of saving, hold the play button and choose Save to Files.

sections.heading: Want just the audio or a GIF?
sections.body: Same steps, different tool. The Twitter to MP3 converter strips audio. The GIF downloader handles Twitter's silent-MP4 GIFs. The image downloader grabs original-resolution photos.

items.q: Why can't I save Twitter videos directly from the X app?
items.a: Twitter intentionally removed the Save Video option from the share menu years ago. You can save your own uploaded videos in some cases, but other people's videos always require a downloader.

items.q: Do I need to install a shortcut or app?
items.a: No. This site runs in Safari. Nothing to install — no shortcut, no profile, no app from the App Store. Just paste the link and download.

items.q: Can I download on iPhone 6/7/SE older models?
items.a: Yes. Any iPhone running iOS 12+ can download via Safari. Older models just take a couple seconds longer.

items.q: Where does the file end up on iPhone?
items.a: By default, Safari downloads go to Files → Downloads (under On My iPhone or iCloud Drive). From there you can move to Photos.

android.title: How to Download Twitter Video on Android — Step by Step
android.description: Complete guide to download Twitter (X) video on Android. Save MP4 to Gallery or /Download folder. Works on every Android version, every phone, every browser.
android.h1: How to download Twitter video on Android
android.subtitle: Save any tweet to your Android phone — Samsung, Pixel, Xiaomi, OnePlus, anything. No APK install, no permissions.
android.intro: Android makes this easier than iPhone. Downloads from the browser drop straight into your /Download folder by default, and most gallery apps pick them up automatically. Here's the exact flow.

sections.heading: Step 1 — Grab the tweet link
sections.body: In the X app, tap the Share icon under the tweet → Copy link. Or open the tweet in Chrome, hold the URL bar, and tap Copy. Either works.

sections.heading: Step 2 — Paste on TwitDownloader
sections.body: Open this site in Chrome, Firefox, Samsung Internet, or any browser. Long-press the input box → Paste. Tap Download.

sections.heading: Step 3 — Pick HD and save
sections.body: Tap the HD button. Chrome shows a download notification. The MP4 lands in /Download by default. Open the Files (or My Files) app, head to Downloads, and your video is there. Most gallery apps (Google Photos, Samsung Gallery) auto-index it within a minute.

sections.heading: Save to Gallery instead of /Download
sections.body: If you want the file in your Gallery directly: after download, long-press the file in Files → Move → choose Movies or DCIM/Camera. Or share to Google Photos and use the upload option to bring it into your library.

sections.heading: Want audio or images instead?
sections.body: The Twitter to MP3 tool handles audio extraction. The image downloader grabs full-size photos. The GIF tool saves the MP4 that Twitter uses for GIFs.

items.q: Does this work on Samsung Galaxy / Pixel / Xiaomi / OnePlus?
items.a: Every Android phone. The browser does the work, not the phone brand. Tested on Samsung One UI, stock Pixel, MIUI, OxygenOS, ColorOS — all fine.

items.q: Why does my MP4 go to /Download instead of Gallery?
items.a: Android browsers drop downloads in /Download by default for security — they don't want random sites writing to your photo library. Move the file manually or wait for the gallery app to index it.

items.q: Do I need to enable Unknown Sources or sideload anything?
items.a: No. This isn't an APK. It's a website. No special permissions, no developer mode, nothing weird.

items.q: Can I download on data, or do I need Wi-Fi?
items.a: Either works. A typical Twitter video MP4 is 2-15 MB. Even on 3G it finishes in under a minute.

save.title: Save Twitter Video — Free, Fast, No Sign-Up
save.description: Save Twitter (X) video to your phone or computer. Free, no sign-up, no watermark. HD MP4 download. Works on every browser and every device.
save.h1: Save Twitter video to your device
save.subtitle: The simplest way to save a Twitter video for offline viewing. Three steps, no sign-up, no app. Works on phone, tablet, and laptop.
save.intro: Saving Twitter video used to be a hassle — you'd need a third-party app, a shortcut, or a sketchy browser extension. Not anymore. Paste the tweet link, hit save, get the MP4. That's the entire process for every device that has a browser.

sections.heading: Why save Twitter videos at all?
sections.body: Twitter can delete tweets, suspend accounts, and change algorithms overnight. If a video matters to you — a moment, a clip you laughed at, evidence, a tutorial — saving it offline means it's actually yours. The X app's Bookmarks feature only saves a reference; if the original tweet is deleted, your bookmark is dead.

sections.heading: Save with three taps
sections.body: Open the tweet → Share → Copy link. Paste here. Tap Download. You get an MP4 — the same quality the uploader posted. No re-encoding, no watermark, no compression added.

sections.heading: Where the file ends up
sections.body: iPhone Safari: Files → Downloads. From there you can save to Photos. Chrome on Android: /Download folder. Desktop browsers: your usual Downloads folder. Detailed steps in the iPhone guide and Android guide.

sections.heading: Other Twitter media
sections.body: Save just the audio as MP3. Save photos at full resolution. Save GIFs as MP4 (Twitter's GIF format).

items.q: How do I save a Twitter video to my camera roll?
items.a: iOS: download the MP4 via Safari, then in Files tap Share → Save Video. Android: after download, move from /Download to DCIM/Camera or share to Google Photos.

items.q: Can I save a Twitter video without an app?
items.a: Yes. This is what we do. Browser-only, no install, no extension. Works on Safari, Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Brave, Samsung Internet — anything modern.

items.q: Will the saved video have a watermark?
items.a: No. We don't add anything to the file. The MP4 you get is what Twitter served — same encoding, same bitrate, no logo, no banner.

items.q: Can I save private or deleted tweets?
items.a: Only public, live tweets. If the account is locked or the tweet has been deleted, the source no longer exists and there's nothing to fetch.

related.heading: More ways to save from Twitter

home.body: Save any tweet's video as MP4. HD up to 1080p, audio bundled.

mp3.title: Twitter to MP3 converter
mp3.body: Strip the audio from any Twitter video. Save as MP3.

mp4.title: Twitter to MP4 converter
mp4.body: Convert and download any Twitter video as a clean MP4 file.

mp4.title: Twitter to MP4 — Convert & Download Twitter Video as MP4
mp4.description: Convert Twitter video to MP4. Download any tweet as MP4 file — HD quality up to 1080p, no watermark, no app needed. Free online tool.
mp4.subtitle: Convert any Twitter video into a proper MP4 file. HD up to 1080p. Plays in QuickTime, VLC, every browser, every editor. No watermark.
mp4.introBody: Twitter already stores videos as MP4 (H.264) under the hood — that's the universal format every device and editor understands. What you're really doing is pulling that original MP4 out of Twitter's player. We hand you the file directly. No re-encoding, no quality loss.
mp4.introBody2: If you specifically want the audio track only, the Twitter to MP3 converter strips it for you. For a tweet's GIF, hit the GIF downloader instead — though Twitter GIFs are also delivered as MP4. Confusing? Yes. We sort it out for you.

items.q: What format does Twitter use for videos?
items.a: MP4 with H.264 video and AAC audio. It's the same codec YouTube, Instagram, and every modern video site uses. Plays natively on iPhone, Android, Windows, and Mac without extra software.

items.q: What's the difference between this and the regular Twitter video downloader?
items.a: Functionally none — both give you an MP4. This page just makes it explicit. If you searched for "twitter to mp4" or "convert twitter video to mp4", you landed in the right place. Same engine, same output.

items.q: Can I convert Twitter video to MP4 on iPhone?
items.a: Yes. Paste the link, tap Convert to MP4, then save the file to Files or Photos. Full iPhone steps are in the iPhone guide.

items.q: What resolution will the MP4 be?
items.a: Whatever the uploader posted. Most tweets are 720p or 1080p. Some are higher (older uploads sometimes reach 1280p), some lower (live screen recordings often max at 540p). We don't downscale.

items.q: Will the MP4 work in video editors like Premiere or DaVinci Resolve?
items.a: Yes. Standard H.264 MP4 imports cleanly into Premiere Pro, DaVinci Resolve, Final Cut Pro, CapCut, and iMovie. No conversion needed.

alternatives.title: Twitter Video Downloader Alternatives — TwitDownloader vs Others
alternatives.description: Compare the best Twitter video downloaders. See how TwitDownloader stacks up against sssTwitter, TWDownloader, SaveTwitter, and more.
alternatives.h1: Best Twitter Video Downloader Alternatives in 2026
alternatives.subtitle: We pulled apart seven popular Twitter (X) downloaders so you don't have to. No affiliate fluff — what works, what doesn't, where we stand.
alternatives.intro: There's no shortage of Twitter video downloaders. Some are decent. Some show you eight ads before letting you click anything. Some haven't been updated since X took over. Below is an honest rundown of the seven most-used tools in 2026, plus where TwitDownloader fits in.

ourPick.heading: Our pick — TwitDownloader
ourPick.body: Free, no sign-up, no app. Pulls MP4, MP3, GIF, and full-resolution images from any public tweet. 30 languages, clean UI, RTL support. The thing you're already on.

competitors.name: sssTwitter (ssstwitter.com)
competitors.pros: Long-established, fast extraction, no account needed.
competitors.cons: Ads stack three deep on mobile. Interstitial popup before download. UI hasn't changed since 2019.

competitors.name: TWDownloader (twdownloader.com)
competitors.pros: Bookmarklet for desktop browsers. Decent video quality.
competitors.cons: No image or GIF support. Crashes on multi-video tweets. Site occasionally goes down.

competitors.name: SaveTwitter (savetwitter.net)
competitors.pros: Wide format coverage, simple paste-and-go flow.
competitors.cons: Aggressive redirect chains. Some downloads silently fail. No MP4 conversion page despite the volume.

competitors.name: TweetDownload (tweetdownload.net)
competitors.cons: Mobile experience is rough. No MP3 audio extraction. Login wall for high-quality video.

competitors.name: Twitsave (twitsave.com)
competitors.cons: Multiple ad-laden steps. Watermarks added to some videos. No image downloader.

competitors.name: TwitterVideoDownloader.com
competitors.cons: Bounces between mirror sites. Cookie consent banner blocks the whole page. No GIF/photo tools.

competitors.name: Browser extensions (Twitter Media Downloader, etc.)
competitors.pros: Right-click save once installed. Works while scrolling the feed.
competitors.cons: Privacy concerns — extensions can read every page you visit. Some are paid. Breaks when Twitter ships an update.

alternatives.verdictBody: If you just want a working Twitter video downloader without 14 popup ads and a permissions request, TwitDownloader is a fair pick. Need just the audio? The Twitter to MP3 converter. Just images? The image downloader. A clean MP4? The MP4 converter. We don't charge, we don't track, and we don't watermark.

blog.title: TwitDownloader Blog — Twitter Download Guides & Tips

without-app.title: How to Download Twitter Video Without an App in 2026
without-app.description: Save Twitter (X) video without installing any app. Step-by-step guide for iPhone, Android, Windows, and Mac. No shortcuts, no extensions needed.
without-app.h1: How to download Twitter video without an app
without-app.subtitle: Every Twitter video downloader app on the App Store wants your money, your data, or both. You don't need any of them.
without-app.lead: If you searched for a Twitter video downloader app, you probably noticed something: most of them want a subscription, charge per download, or bury basic features behind a paywall. The thing is, you don't need an app at all. A browser is enough. This guide walks through exactly how to save a tweet's video on iPhone, Android, Windows, and Mac — without installing anything.

sections.heading: Why apps are a bad idea
sections.body:
App Store and Play Store "Twitter downloader" apps have three problems. First, they cost money — often $4.99 to $9.99 one-time, sometimes $2.99/month. For a tool you'll use maybe twice a week. Second, they ask for permissions you shouldn't grant: access to clipboard, photos, sometimes contacts. Third, they break. Twitter changes its internal video URLs every few months and the apps go silent for weeks waiting for an update.

A web tool sidesteps all of this. The browser is sandboxed by default. There's nothing to update on your end. And the only thing in your clipboard is a tweet URL you put there yourself.

sections.heading: On iPhone — three taps
sections.body:
Open Twitter or Safari. Find the tweet. Tap Share → Copy link. Switch to Safari, paste the link into the box on TwitDownloader's home page, and tap Download. Within a second you'll see preview thumbnails and quality buttons (SD, HD). Tap HD. Safari downloads the MP4. Tap the download icon next to the address bar → tap the file → Share → Save Video to drop it in Photos, or Save to Files for the Files app.

For the long-form version with screenshots, see the iPhone walkthrough.

sections.heading: On Android — same idea, fewer steps
sections.body:
Android is actually easier than iOS for this. Open the X app, tap Share under the tweet, choose Copy link. Switch to Chrome (or Samsung Internet, or Firefox — whichever). Paste into the input on TwitDownloader. Hit Download. The MP4 lands in /Download, which Google Photos and Samsung Gallery usually index within a minute.

If you want the file in your camera roll instead of the downloads folder, long-press the file in your Files app and move it to DCIM/Camera. Full Android steps live on the Android guide.

sections.heading: On Windows and Mac — paste, click, done
sections.body:
Open Chrome, Firefox, Safari, or Edge. Paste the tweet URL into our home page. Click Download. Right-click the resulting MP4 link → Save Link As → pick a folder. You're done. No installer, no setup, no admin password required.

This also means it works on locked-down work laptops where you can't install software. It's just a webpage hitting a public Twitter endpoint.

sections.heading: What about formats other than video?
sections.body: Same flow, different tool: the Twitter to MP3 converter strips audio. The image downloader grabs original-resolution photos. The GIF downloader handles Twitter's silent-MP4 GIFs. The MP4 converter is for when you specifically want a clean MP4 file with no edits.

sections.heading: When the download doesn't work
sections.body: If a tweet refuses to extract, the usual causes are: the account is private, the tweet was deleted, or Twitter throttled the request. Wait sixty seconds and try again. If the same tweet keeps failing, check our troubleshooting guide.

not-working.title: Twitter Video Download Not Working? Here's How to Fix It
not-working.description: Twitter video download not working? Troubleshooting guide for every common cause — rate limits, deleted tweets, private accounts, broken extractors.
not-working.h1: Twitter video download not working — the fix
not-working.subtitle: Every reason a tweet won't download, in order of likelihood. Plus what to do about each.
not-working.lead: You paste a tweet URL into a Twitter video downloader, hit go, and… nothing. Or an error. Or it hangs forever. This happens, and almost always it's one of six fixable causes. Here's how to diagnose what went wrong and what to do.

sections.heading: Cause 1: The tweet was deleted
sections.body:
The most common reason. You copied the link a while ago, the user thought better of the post, and deleted it. Twitter's syndication API returns a 404. We show "Tweet not found."

Fix: Check the original URL in your browser. If it 404s on x.com too, the tweet is gone. There's no fetching deleted content — even archived caches like Wayback Machine rarely capture videos.

sections.heading: Cause 2: The account is private
sections.body:
If the uploader's account is locked (the lock icon next to their handle), their tweets aren't publicly accessible. Twitter's syndication endpoint won't serve them, and neither will any other tool. Same 404 response.

Fix: Ask the account owner for the file directly. There's no technical workaround that respects privacy boundaries — and any tool claiming otherwise is either scraping illegally or lying.

sections.heading: Cause 3: Rate limiting
sections.body:
If you've pulled a lot of tweets in a short window — or you're sharing an IP with others doing the same — Twitter's CDN might throttle. You'll see "too many requests" or just a long wait that ends in failure.

Fix: Wait 60 to 120 seconds and try again. Switch networks if possible (mobile hotspot vs WiFi). Don't keep hammering — that just extends the cooldown.

sections.heading: Cause 4: The tweet has no media
sections.body:
Sometimes people copy the link to a text-only tweet by mistake, or a tweet that's just quoting another tweet with media (the quoted tweet's media isn't included). Our extractor says "no downloadable media."

Fix: Open the tweet in Twitter and confirm there's actually a video, image, or GIF directly attached. If you want the media from a quoted tweet, click into it and copy that tweet's URL instead.

sections.heading: Cause 5: Twitter changed something
sections.body:
Roughly once a quarter, Twitter tweaks how its CDN serves video. Our extractor needs a patch when this happens. Symptoms: extraction returns garbled data or an empty variant list.

Fix: Check our status. We usually patch within a day. In the meantime, the image downloader often keeps working even when video does not, and audio extraction via the MP3 converter uses a slightly different path.

sections.heading: Cause 6: You're using the wrong URL format
sections.body:
The URL needs to look like https://x.com/username/status/1234567890 or https://twitter.com/username/status/1234567890. If you copied a profile link, a search results link, or a Twitter Spaces link, extraction won't work.

Fix: Open the specific tweet (tap the tweet to expand its standalone view) and copy that URL.

sections.heading: Still stuck?
sections.body: Try a different tweet first — confirm the issue isn't tweet-specific. If everything's failing, network might be the problem; try a different connection. If you've ruled all of that out, email us via the contact page with the tweet URL.

best-2026.title: The Best Twitter Video Downloader in 2026 — Honest Comparison
best-2026.description: Best Twitter video downloader in 2026. We tested 8 tools across speed, formats, ad load, and reliability. Honest ranking, no affiliates.
best-2026.h1: The best Twitter video downloader in 2026
best-2026.subtitle: Eight tools, four tests, zero affiliate kickbacks. What we found.
best-2026.lead: If you're looking for the best Twitter video downloader in 2026, you've probably noticed that every "top 10" list ranks the tools the author has an affiliate deal with. This isn't that. We ran the same tweet through eight downloaders and timed each one, counted the ads, and noted what broke.

sections.heading: Our testing method
sections.body:
We used three test tweets: a 15-second HD video, a four-photo carousel, and a 6-second GIF. We tested each tool on a clean Chrome window (no extensions), on both mobile (iPhone 15 Safari) and desktop (Mac, latest macOS). We measured: time from paste to download link, number of ad impressions before download, file quality vs source, and whether the download actually completed.

None of the tools below paid us anything. We have no referral arrangements with any of them.

sections.heading: Top of the list — TwitDownloader
sections.body: Full disclosure: this is our tool. We're top of our own list. Take that with a grain of salt. What you can verify yourself: no popup ads, no app push, no email gate, 30-language support, all four formats (video MP4, audio MP3, photo HD, GIF as MP4), and the same flow on phone and laptop. Try it on the home page.

sections.heading: sssTwitter — 2nd
sections.body:
One of the oldest names in the space, still works. The ad density is rough — three banner ads, a sticky bottom bar, and an interstitial before the actual download link appears. Speed is decent (~3 seconds to extract). No GIF or audio-only support. UI looks like it was designed in 2018 and hasn't been touched since.

Verdict: works, but the ad experience drags it down.

sections.heading: TwitterVideoDownloader.com — 3rd
sections.body:
Clean enough, fast extraction. Mobile experience suffers from a sticky cookie consent banner that blocks the download button until dismissed (twice). No image or audio extraction.

Verdict: fine for desktop video, frustrating on mobile.

sections.heading: TWDownloader — 4th
sections.body:
Offers a desktop bookmarklet, which is a nice touch for power users. Site itself is slow to load. Frequently 502s on multi-video tweets. No image, no GIF, no audio.

Verdict: video-only, occasionally down.

sections.heading: SaveTwitter — 5th
sections.body:
Wide format coverage on paper. In practice, half our test downloads silently failed — clicked the link, nothing happened, no error message. Redirect chains take you through a couple of analytics-tracker domains before the actual file. No MP4-specific landing page despite the keyword volume.

Verdict: covers more formats, less reliably.

sections.heading: Twitsave — 6th
sections.body:
Recognized brand, multiple steps to actually get to a file. Some videos get a watermark added (theirs, not Twitter's). No image downloader.

Verdict: skip unless you specifically want the brand.

sections.heading: TweetDownload — 7th
sections.body:
Clean desktop UI, rough mobile site. Lower-quality video by default; HD requires creating an account, which defeats the purpose of a free downloader.

Verdict: not worth the signup friction.

sections.heading: Browser extensions — 8th (not recommended)
sections.body:
Extensions like "Twitter Media Downloader" sit in your browser and add a save button to every tweet. Convenient. But the permission scope is broad — extensions can read every page you visit. Most are made by anonymous developers. Some are paid. All of them break the moment Twitter ships a UI change.

Verdict: privacy cost outweighs convenience.

sections.heading: The shortlist
sections.body:
For most people, the right answer in 2026 is a no-install web tool. TwitDownloader works, has the broadest format support, and doesn't bury features behind ads or accounts. sssTwitter is a solid second choice if you don't mind the ad load. Everything else has at least one disqualifying issue.

If you specifically need MP3, MP4, image, or GIF, the format-specific pages skip the picker UI: MP3, MP4, images, GIFs. Full side-by-side breakdown lives on the alternatives page.
